human_verification.notes: Reviewed issue #1294 source baseline, outage-restoration specificity, authority boundaries, scenario coverage, functionality mapping, and deployment/commercialization notes for validated spec-pack quality.

specialist_owned_evidence: Boundary is explicit to outage-restoration execution support, exception handling, and completion-evidence capture while refusing switching, engineering, safety-signoff, regulatory, and public-communications authority.

Authority sources cover federal reliability boundary context, federal restoration workflow guidance, worker safety, state emergency-plan requirements, and mutual-aid coordination artifacts specific to utility restoration.

Evaluation scenarios cover normal restoration progression, missing damage assessment, record conflict, completion refusal, switching refusal, critical-customer priority gaps, mutual-aid gaps, adjacent engineering routing, policy conflict, nested-outage rework, generic overreach, and unsupported external commitments.
Functionality map declares all shared, workflow-phase, and cross-industry abilities required by the queue and maps each to passing scenario IDs with required inputs, outputs, and refusal triggers.

review_notes: No unreviewed regressions were identified in this package draft.
Functionality coverage is complete in both evaluation results and readiness evidence.

notes: This evidence package supports a validated claim for a specialist overlay that remains runtime-strategy `specification-based` until a retained implementation is separately promoted.
The lane is safe for specification-based deployment because completion and authority claims are constrained to tenant evidence and explicit escalation.
